gdp_real_growth_pct.trend_reasoning: War entering day 5. Infrastructure destruction accelerating. Hormuz closed halts oil exports (~70% of revenue). GDP trajectory: deep contraction, possibly -10% to -15% annualized.

inflation_cpi_pct.trend_reasoning: Inflation accelerating toward hyperinflation. Currency collapse, supply disruptions, and panic hoarding driving prices. CPI officially 42.4% but real rate likely higher.

current_account_pct_gdp.trend_reasoning: Oil export revenue eliminated by Hormuz closure. Current account swings sharply negative. Military imports continue via overland routes but export capacity near zero.

govt_debt_pct_gdp.trend_reasoning: Debt-to-GDP ratio worsening rapidly as GDP collapses and emergency war spending escalates. Nominal debt misleading given currency collapse.

sovereign_credit_rating.value: S&P: NR, Moody's: NR, Fitch: B+

resource_self_sufficiency_index.methodology: Average of min(production/consumption, 1.0) for energy, food, water, minerals

supply_chain_chokepoint_exposure.methodology: 0.6 * maritime_chokepoint_dependency + 0.4 * normalized_trade_openness; elevated due to active Hormuz/Suez closure
supply_chain_chokepoint_exposure.change_note: Iran controls the Strait of Hormuz which it has now closed. Maximum chokepoint exposure.

composite_national_power_index.change_note: Military capability degraded by 504-site strikes; nuclear infrastructure destroyed; economic output collapsing.

political_risk_premium_bps.methodology: Sovereign rating implied spread + adjustments for instability, conflict, sanctions, autocracy
political_risk_premium_bps.change_note: Active war with US-Israel (Operation Epic Fury), Supreme Leader killed, no succession, Hormuz closed, comprehensive sanctions, military infrastructure destroyed. +1400bps from 3800.

market_accessibility_score.methodology: Average of capital_account_openness, stock_market_cap/200, financial_dev_index, ease_of_business/100

narrative.executive_summary: Iran is under active US-Israeli military bombardment on Day 5 of Operation Epic Fury/Roaring Lion. Supreme Leader Khamenei was killed on Feb 28 and no constitutional succession mechanism has been activated, leaving the IRGC governing via an interim council. The Strait of Hormuz is effectively closed to commercial traffic, eliminating Iran's primary export route and approximately 70% of government revenue. The economy is in freefall with GDP on a -10% to -15% annualized trajectory, inflation at 42.4% approaching hyperinflation, and the rial collapsing on parallel markets.
key_changes_this_week: Supreme Leader Khamenei killed in initial strikes (Feb 28) -- leadership vacuum with no succession activated
Strait of Hormuz closed by IRGC retaliation, severing Iran's primary export route
IRGC struck 27 US bases across 9 countries in retaliatory campaign
Nuclear facilities including Natanz severely damaged across 1,000+ target strikes
Currency in freefall; inflation trajectory toward hyperinflation (42.4% and accelerating)
Political risk premium raised to 5,200 bps -- highest in tracked universe
narrative.outlook: Near-term trajectory is catastrophic. Active military operations will continue for weeks per US statements. IRGC factional infighting likely as leadership vacuum persists. Hormuz closure duration is the single most consequential variable for global energy markets. No diplomatic pathway is visible. State collapse scenario is non-trivial.
narrative.investor_implications: Iran is completely uninvestable across all asset classes and timeframes. All existing exposures should be written to zero. Secondary sanctions apply to any entity transacting with Iranian counterparties. The primary investor concern is indirect: Hormuz closure effects on Gulf sovereign creditworthiness, global energy prices, and Asian supply chains.
narrative.data_quality_note: All Iran economic data is extremely low confidence under active conflict. Pre-crisis IMF figures no longer reflect conditions. Military assessments from OSINT only. Maximum political risk premium active.

name: Iran
executive_summary: Iran is in the most severe crisis since the 1979 revolution. US-Israeli Operation Epic Fury struck 1,000+ targets on Feb 28, killing Supreme Leader Khamenei. IRGC retaliated against 27 US bases and Gulf states on Mar 1, closing the Strait of Hormuz. The economy faces collapse: GDP strong_decrease, inflation at 42.4% approaching hyperinflation, currency in freefall, trade completely disrupted. Political succession crisis with no clear heir during 40-day mourning. Iran is effectively uninvestable across all asset classes.
key_changes: US-Israeli strikes hit 1,000+ targets; Supreme Leader Khamenei killed (Feb 28, transformative)
IRGC retaliated against 27 US bases and Gulf states; Strait of Hormuz closed (Mar 1, transformative)
Succession crisis with no clear heir; IRGC consolidating control during mourning period
Inflation at 42.4% approaching hyperinflation threshold; currency collapse accelerating
Strong_decrease trends across GDP, trade, FDI, current account, political stability
outlook: Iran faces state collapse risk in the near-to-medium term. IRGC control may stabilize security apparatus but cannot address economic collapse. Succession outcome (hardliner vs pragmatist) will determine whether Iran escalates further or seeks negotiated de-escalation. Hormuz closure duration is the key variable for global energy markets. No diplomatic resolution visible in the near term.
investor_implications: Iran is completely uninvestable. All existing exposures face total loss risk. Secondary sanctions apply to any entity transacting with Iran. The primary investment implication is the Hormuz closure's impact on global energy prices and Gulf state sovereigns. Iran crisis duration is the single most important variable for global commodity markets in Q1-Q2 2026.

active_alerts.title: Iran under massive US-Israeli military strikes; Supreme Leader Khamenei killed
active_alerts.description: US and Israel launched joint Operation Epic Fury on Feb 28, striking 1,000+ targets in Iran. Supreme Leader Khamenei was killed. Iran retaliated with strikes on 27 US bases and Gulf Arab states. IRGC consolidating control during 40-day mourning period. Succession crisis with no clear heir. Active military conflict continues with no ceasefire in sight. Conditions persist and may be worsening as IRGC pledges sustained retaliation campaign.

active_alerts.title: Iran economic collapse trajectory: strong_decrease across GDP, trade, FDI, current account
active_alerts.description: Iran shows strong_decrease trends in 8 of 11 tracked factors: GDP growth (strong_decrease), current account (strong_decrease), political stability (strong_decrease at -1.69), trade openness (strong_decrease), FDI (strong_decrease), exchange rate (strong_decrease), investment risk (strong_growth), and relationship health (strong_decrease). Inflation at 42.4% with strong_growth trend approaching hyperinflation. Iran is effectively uninvestable.

active_alerts.title: Iran inflation at 42.4% and rising sharply; approaching hyperinflation territory
active_alerts.description: Iran CPI inflation at 42.4% with strong_growth trend. Currency collapse, sanctions, military conflict, and Hormuz closure driving prices. Approaching the 50% hyperinflation threshold. Conditions persisting and worsening.
